items.title: Florida SNAP food restrictions take effect April 20, 2026: soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts banned
items.summary: Starting April 20, 2026, Florida SNAP recipients will no longer be able to purchase soda, energy drinks (65+ mg caffeine per 8 oz), candy, or prepared desserts with EBT benefits. The waiver remains in effect for two years through April 19, 2028. Coffee and tea are not affected.

items.title: Colorado SNAP Healthy Choice Waiver restricts soft drink purchases starting April 30, 2026
items.summary: Colorado will restrict SNAP purchases of soft drinks starting April 30, 2026. The restriction covers sodas, sweetened iced teas, energy drinks, sports drinks, and beverages with less than 50% fruit/vegetable juice. Water, unsweetened seltzers, milk, and 100% juice remain purchasable.

items.title: KFF: Medicaid provider-tax waiver limits may force changes in some states as early as April 1, 2026
items.summary: KFF reports that new limits on CMS 'uniformity waivers' for provider taxes could require some states to revise provider taxes as early as April 1, 2026, with at least seven states estimated to be affected (including CA, IL, MA, MI, OH, NY, and WV). The 2025 reconciliation law's Medicaid changes are projected to reduce federal Medicaid spending by $911 billion over 10 years.

items.title: KFF: New Medicaid work requirements for expansion adults slated to start Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: KFF says the 2025 reconciliation law creates new work requirements for Medicaid enrollees in the ACA expansion group starting January 1, 2027. KFF estimates more than half (5.3 million) of people newly uninsured from these Medicaid changes would be tied to the new work requirements.

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens effective Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS is preparing states for an upcoming change that will limit the ability to claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals. Exceptions include emergency medical conditions and lawfully residing children and pregnant people under state options.

items.title: CMS: Medicaid/CHIP enrollment at 75.7 million in December 2025, down 3.7 million from December 2024
items.summary: December 2025 data shows 68.5 million individuals enrolled in Medicaid and 7.2 million in CHIP, for a total of 75.7 million. Enrollment dropped about 1.0% from November 2025 (727,000 people) and is down 4.7% from December 2024.

items.title: IRS deadline: April 15, 2026 is the federal filing and payment deadline for 2025 tax returns
items.summary: The federal deadline to file 2025 individual income tax returns (Form 1040) and pay taxes owed is April 15, 2026. Taxpayers can request an extension to October 15, 2026 for filing only — taxes are still due April 15. EITC/ACTC refunds were available by March 2 for most early filers who used direct deposit.

items.title: Pell Grant 2026-27: maximum award set at $7,395; new hard SAI cutoff at $14,790
items.summary: For the 2026-27 award year (July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027), the maximum Pell Grant is $7,395 and the minimum is $740. Congress locked in a new rule blocking Pell eligibility for students whose Student Aid Index (SAI) is $14,790 or higher. The FAFSA federal deadline is June 30, 2027.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI maximum is $994/month (individual) and $1,491/month (couple)
items.summary: SSA confirmed a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 affecting about 75 million Americans. The federal SSI payment standard is $994/month for individuals and $1,491/month for couples. Disability work thresholds are $1,690/month (non-blind SGA) and $2,830/month (blind SGA).

items.title: Federal Register: ACF proposes reallotment of FY2025 LIHEAP funds; comments due April 30, 2026
items.summary: The Administration for Children and Families (ACF) proposed reallotting unspent FY2025 LIHEAP funds to jurisdictions that received FY2026 direct LIHEAP awards. The Federal Register notice lists a comment period ending April 30, 2026.

items.title: WIC: FY2026 fruit/veg cash benefit set at $26 (kids), $48 (preg/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding); April 2026 compliance deadline
items.summary: USDA FNS set FY2026 monthly WIC cash-value voucher amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. States must authorize at least one form of fruits/vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026.

items.title: HUD posts CY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher administrative fee rates; 88% proration for early 2026
items.summary: HUD's CY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher administrative fee rate description says advanced admin fees for January-March 2026 were at 88% proration. Mainstream voucher renewal funding is now included with the regular HCV program starting CY 2026. PHAs are urged to plan for potential 2026 funding constraints.
